name: Miles Davis
description: American jazz trumpeter, bandleader, and composer (1926–1991)
abstract: Miles Dewey Davis III (May 26, 1926 – September 28, 1991) was an American trumpeter, bandleader and composer. He is among the most influential and acclaimed figures in the history of jazz and 20th-century music.

concerts.label: Miles Davis with Gil Evans and Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: Miles is getting ready to start a new band... I called him up and I said, 'Hey Miles, it’s Harold.'... 'I got this little tour, I want to see if you want to make it.'... So I came off the bandstand and he would throw a punch at me... That meant you were okay... so he called me and told me where to p
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Herbie Hancock
about_quotes.evidence: I had been a former member of Miles’ band... I was there for five and a half years after that... I learned so much from Miles that I would be a very different piano player had it not been for that experience in playing with that particular Miles Davis group
about_quotes.doc_title: Herbie Hancock interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, November 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Vince DiMartino
about_quotes.evidence: he said, “Hey Bob, you know my son’s been playing trumpet and I think he sounds pretty good, so is there any recordings that I should have, I should get him?” And we didn’t have a lot of money. So he named like four recordings and it was Miles Davis...
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince DiMartino interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 8, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola
about_quotes.evidence: I had been listening to the records... when I heard he was coming to the Town Casino of course I showed up... I was very disappointed at first, but then when I heard them I said, well yeah, this might be okay
about_quotes.doc_title: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 30, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: So Miles validated me, and after Miles recorded Stablemates, people started recording my stuff... So I owe it to my dear friend John Coltrane, who took it, and Miles, who recorded it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Highlights include his tenure with Miles Davis from 1963-68... We’d been off about four months, and he told us this is a benefit... I was the spokesperson of the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 17, 1999
